entry.title: Re: How to extend the SAP Gateway log in TCODE /n/IWFND/E...
entry.content: Thank you very much! I was able to configure and see the logs from the past days!

entry.title: Re: How to pass the Order Quantity Value in Migration Tem...
entry.content:
Hi @Garima5 

Every migration object internally calls BAPI to create data in the system. The object mentioned uses BAPI_CONTRACT_CREATEFROMDATA to create sales contract data in the system. Could you please open a case to SAP so that we can check in which coding did this error return.

Best regards,
Irene

entry.title: How to create outbound delivery during BD3/BDK (Third party sales process) in SAP public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi team,

Currently, based on our company's business needs, we are considering using the BD3 or BDK process for scenarios where third-party vendors directly ship goods to customers. In the standard BD3 or BDK process, creating an outbound delivery is not involved, as the goods are shipped directly from the vendor. However, from the company's perspective, we still need to create delivery documents.

Are there any other steps or settings we can adjust to generate these delivery documents? Would it be feasible to add a delivery note step to this process?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Extensibility

entry.title: Re: Aggregation on Data Action
entry.content: @William_Yu, I’ve identified the root cause. The actual result should be the product of the sum of units and the sum of lookup price. However, the inaccurate value appears because the calculation is performed row-wise, multiplying units by lookup price for each dimension and then summing the results, which leads to incorrect aggregation. Can you help me correct this issue? How can I ensure the calculation follows the correct aggregation logic in SAP Analytics Cloud?

entry.title: Re: SAP Partner Sales Academy
entry.content: @philroyl Is this still active? do we have the SAP Academy Sales/Presales Certification for 2025 for existing partners of SAP? I appreciate your kind response.

entry.title: Re: SAP DM Plugin and production process sync call
entry.content:
Hi,

Were you able to call the production process API in synchronous mode. I have the same issue and need to understand the solution.

Prasanna Rampurmath

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA Extended Warehouse Management Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Options
entry.content:
Summary

The document discusses how to choose between different KPI monitoring tools for SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM). It presents three main options: integrated EWM capabilities with Fiori applications, the cloud-based SAP Warehouse Insights, and the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). The post provides criteria for selecting the best solution based on IT resources, data volume, dashboard requirements, and the use of SAP BTP.

 

Business Requirements

Implementing SAP Extended Warehouse Management for a running warehouse solution always goes hand in hand with a KPI driven logistics department. The purpose of this post is to determine the most suitable KPI monitoring and analytics solution for a customers EWM, specifically when transitioning to or migrating to a newer version of SAP  EWM on S/4. The customer needs to ensure that existing KPI capabilities are maintained or enhanced in the new system. This involves deciding how and where KPIs should be displayed and managed in EWM.

 

Solution Options

The following SAP solutions can cover your requirements:

  • SAP Extended Warehouse Management Integrated Capabilities

  • SAP Warehouse Insights on BTP

  • SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) on BTP with BW4/HANA

 

Characterization of the Solution Options

Option #1:    SAP Extended Warehouse Management Integrated Capabilities

The following options come with a standard EWM deployment.

EWM has lots of data available in the warehouse management monitor (Transaction /SCWM/MON), which can be leveraged. It also has the possibility to design KPIs with the KPI designer, the easy graphics framework (EGF) as well as the Fiori application warehouse KPIs. 

The Fiori Application Warehouse KPIs has a standard set of KPIs which can be adjusted to your needs.

This could be done by IT experienced business colleagues and thus it offers more flexibility than the solutions which have to be designed and implemented together with IT departments.

As mentioned, the integrated Capabilities are distributed over multiple tools in EWM and are not available in e.g. one specific dashboard.

Option #2:    SAP Warehouse Insights on BTP

SAP Warehouse Insights is a cloud based real-time warehouse analytics and optimization tool. It leverages historical data and focuses on resources and travel distances in the warehouse.

It leverages the data and visualizes warehouse activities while focusing on reducing empty travel time including the optimization of warehouse resources and corresponding workload. It does real-time optimizations for resources and also presents the results in sophisticated resource KPI dashboards.

Please note that SAP Warehouse Insights requires an own license.

Option #3:    SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) on BTP with BW4/HANA

With SAP Analytics Cloud you’re free to configure your KPIs including various dashboards according to your needs. BI Extractors and multiple available APIs enable SAC for real-time warehouse data and therefor real-time dashboards.

As it’s being hosted in the Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud is well integrable into your future-proof cloud ready system landscape leveraging multiple data sources including your Extended Warehouse management system.

As it’s highly customizable, you can also integrate multiple other data sources, which might be relevant for your logistic distribution centers into your own designed and configured dashboard.

 

Criteria to determine the most suitable target solution

Do you have a strong business department but only a small IT department?

(Option #1) With the integrated capabilities it is possible also for users with no deep IT knowledge to create KPIs according to your business needs. Basis is that Fiori including Analysis Path Framework including the Fiori Application “Manage KPIs and Reports” is also available to the users.

The same is possible for extensions/customizations of the warehouse management monitor (Transaction /SCWM/MON)

Do you have the need to have process high data volumes as well as historical data ?

For maximum scalability and reading historical data we recommend using (Option #3), because with Analytics Cloud you’re able to have the necessary resources in background to process every amount of data

Do you need to have every KPI available? Shall the KPIs be presented in specific Dashboards?

Either with (Option #1) the integrated capabilities of EWM or (Option #3) SAP Analytics Cloud you can design every KPI you can .

Only with SAC it’s possible to have multiple custom dashboards.

Do you have SAP BTP in use? Or do you have plans for its future use?

Without the integration of SAP BTP it’s currently not possible to leverage functionalities of Warehouse Insights (Option #2) or SAP Analytics Cloud (Option #3).

Do you have to reduce travel distances and optimize resource usage?

(Option #2) For analyzing travel distance data according to several constraints and geological data and possible usage of IoT devices – Warehouse Insights is the best possible solution.  

Are you already using SAP Analytics Cloud ?

(Option #3) If you’re using SAP Analytics Cloud in other parts of your company – this is the chance to also leverage the colleagues for creating a top-modern EWM KPI dashboard or multiple dashboards according to the specific needs in dedicated areas of the warehouse.

 

Conclusion

It really depends on which KPIs you’re currently using. While you might be possible to see a lot of data in the warehouse management monitor, you also want to have them on a dashboard in a graphically pleasing way. Therefore, it is recommended to use either SAP Analytics Cloud or Fiori Application Warehouse KPIs. The usage of the latter is also feasible if you do not have a large IT department, so business users can learn to design their own KPIs. If you have a strong IT department and you’re already using SAP Analytics Cloud then the go to approach is investing into modern dashboards which can be customized and adapted to your individual needs. If resource optimization as well as reducing empty travel in the warehouse is a high priority topic for you, then it’s definitely worth having a closer look into SAP Warehouse Insights.

 

Further information

For additional documentation please refer to:

  • Fiori Apps Library for Warehouse KPIs Fiori Application
  • SAP Product Information for Warehouse Insights: SAP Warehouse Insights
  • SAP Product Information for Analytics Cloud: SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Blogpost about Customizing and Extending Warehouse Management Monitors as well as EGF (Option 2 in the Blogpost)
  • SAP Note 3404074 KPI Catalog vs KPI tiles in S/4 HANA Smart Business Apps
  • CDS Views for Fiori Application Warehouse KPIs which can be leveraged to adapt the Fiori Application to your needs
  • Fiori App Manage KPIs and Reports to adapt Fiori Application Warehouse KPIs
  • SAP Note 3195541 How-To Guides for SAP Warehouse Insights

entry.title: Re: "I am not able to take the 'Stay Certified' assessment.
entry.content: What you're talking about is not related to my question. I need access whenever I need it. During the workweek, I don't have time to take the exam because I am working.

entry.title: Re: Need help with CL_GUI_DOCKING_CONTAINER, SIZE BAR.
entry.content:
Thank you.

I thought I could use CL_GUI_DOCKING_CONTAINER's FLOAT Method, but the Help Portal said, "This method is not active," so I thought there was a way.

When creating CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER, I thought Docking Container had that method because if you use CL_GUI_CONTAINER=>SCREEN0 in Parent, the Size Bar does not come out.

entry.title: How do we handle data formatting while downloading result from function module ‘READ_TEXT’ ?
entry.content:
Our requirement is to Mass Change Recipe Process data. Logical sequence to be followed in achieving that is,

  1. We need to convert the Recipe – Process Longtext into formats highlighted by the HTML tags(output of FM READ_TEXT)
  2. Export/download the report(to excel)
  3. Make required changes to the Longtext

Upload the Longtext so that system reads the formatted text and replaces the text formatting with required ITF/HTML tags to upload the data into Recipe Process – Process Elements tab. 

To export Recipe Process Longtext, as we must retrieve long text via FM ‘READ_TEXT’, we have the output as below with tags. Below is an example of a sentence in Longtext extracted from function module READ_TEXT

<P>• Bring racks to the line from chiller/cooler.<br/>•If dicing, grab lugger of diced product you will be mixing. If not dicing, grab lugger of stripped logs for batch you will be mixing.<br/>• Take temperature of a log from the racks.<br/>• <strong>Ifmeat temp is below 32°F. or above 44°F., notify supervisor orFSQR.</strong><div><strong><br/></strong>

In the below Recipe example, For the below line in a Process step longtext, how do we make “Lines 3 / 4 Mixer:” Bold removing the HTML tags – strong in the exported file.

FM READ_TEXT Output : <(><<)>strong>Lines 3 / 4 Mixer:<(><<)>/strong>

And vice versa, how do we read a bold text from any input and add the HTML tags to upload the text into longtexts.

Can someone help on this please?

entry.title: Effort to implement document splitting
entry.content:
Good morning community, can you help me get a clear idea of ​​what requires less effort?

Effort to implement document splitting in a system already converted to S4Hana VS a system still in ERP.

Thank you!!

Francisco J. López

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Four
entry.content:
@Daniel_Davis  Hi, I have a question on my customer's project about the dynamic measures. 

Thinking we have a measure Sales_Amount on 2 dimensions(Product, Plant) and each dimension have 2 values(Product_A, Product_B; Plant_1, Plant_2), and we have another time dimension(Month) .   From your post, I know I can show the selected Product or Plant as dimension, and show the Sales_Amount in Months. 

Now, I want to use Line Chart, and show the Monthly trend of Sales_Amount by selected dimension values. When I selecte 3 values(Product_A, Product_B, Plant_1), I expect 2 lines on the chart(line 1: Product_A and Plant_1, line2:Product_B and Plant_1 ), while when I selecte all the 4 values(Product_A, Product_B, Plant_1, Plant_2), there should be 4 lines(line1:Product_A and Plant_1, line2:Product_A and Plant_2, line3:Product_B and Plant_1, line4:Product_B and Plant_2). 

I think adding Restricted Measures in the Chart Builder panel can show the lines, but in customer project, there are lots of dimensions and dimension values, it's impossible to create all Restricted Measures in the Builder in advance.

So is it possible to dynamic create Restricted Measure in script?  the selected dimension values are filters' values of the Restricted Measure.  

Waiting for your advise, thanks.

entry.title: Master Data replication using IDOC-ALE - No consistency check in target system
entry.content:
For example, ALE project replication (transaction CJAL) using FM BAPI_PROJECT_SAVEREPLICA. First all necessary tables and table entries will be selected. At the end FM ALE_PROJECT_POST will be called:

Within FM ALE_PROJECT_POST modify statement is called on tables in the target system:

 

 

 

  MODIFY PROJ FROM TABLE PROJ_UP

  MODIFY PRPS FROM TABLE PRPS_UP.

  MODIFY PRHI FROM TABLE PRHI_UP.

  MODIFY JSTO FROM TABLE JSTO_UP.

  MODIFY JEST FROM TABLE JEST_UP.

 

 

 

So, this can lead to inconsistencies if source and target system are not fully in sync in terms of customizing and master data.

entry.title: Issue with FMS on Issue for Production Quantity
entry.content:
Hello,

I have created below fms on Issue for Production document which is not getting triggered on its own:

SELECT CASE
WHEN CAST($[$13.1880000004.0] AS INT) = 4
THEN CAST($[$13.87.0] AS DECIMAL(18,4))
ELSE 0.0001
END

Scenario: If the selected row item is a resources or rowtype = 290, then it should populate .0001 in quantity field.

I want this to be triggered when a production order is selected on the issue for production document.

Can anyone please suggest what corrections to make in this fms or suggest a better query to get this fms working?

Regards,

Joseph

entry.title: Please help me to understand the integration process required for Material Ledger in a 2-Tier system
entry.content:
Dear Team

Please help me to understand the integration process required for Material Ledger in a 2-Tier system

2 Tier system, One with SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud and another with S/4 HANA Private cloud.

  1. How does the Material Ledger manage intercompany transactions and transfer pricing in a 2-tier system?
  2. What are the challenges and solutions for synchronizing Material Ledger data between the Private Cloud and Public Cloud in a 2-tier system?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Can SAP PDMI alone move EBOM from Teamcenter to SAP
entry.content:
Can SAP PDMI alone move the EBOM from Teamcenter to SAP or do we need T4EA also.

In chatGpt got this. But sometimes its not 100% correct. So would like to confirm the same.

You can use PDMI alone if:

  • You only need basic product data synchronization (materials, BOMs, classification).
  • You use SAP S/4HANA with Engineering Control Center (ECTR) for CAD data management.
  • Your integration is one-directional (Teamcenter → SAP) with minimal transformations.
  • Your business does not require complex workflow automation.

Please confirm.

entry.title: Why is there no SAP Cloud ALM TDD?
entry.content:
There is a Solution Manager TDD license. But there is SAP Cloud ALM TDD license for partners?
We partners have to train hundreds of consultants and test the releases. Best regards, Christian

entry.title: Re: SAC - List of all classic stories / List of all classic apps

entry.title: Issues with Archiving Batches
entry.content:
Hello,

I’m experiencing issue with archiving batches in SAP system using transaction SARA with the archiving object MM_SPSTOCK. When attempting to archive batches, I encounter the following error messages:

  • MCH1: "Dependent batch records exist"
  • MCHA: "Quality notifications exist

First I tried to archive QM notifications using the QM_QMEL object in test mode, but nothing was archived. I also checked the QMEL table and found two QM notifications associated with the batch. When I reviewed these in QM02, they appear as closed.

 

What should I try next? Has anyone experienced a similar issue? How can I resolve this problem?

entry.title: Re: SAP Screen Personas 3.0 SP18 Flavor freezes
entry.content:
Hi vananko,

thanks for your Comment 🙂

I tried this and went through the individual steps step by step in my script. To explain, the script is a script that calls other scripts.
So I have commented out all scripts. So that only my variable is left and the statement, but everything in the parentheses is commented out.
if(session.utils.idexists(MYVARIABLE){
.
.
.
}
I have also tried to unfreeze the whole thing with an else block by using the command sendVKey(0). and also when I realized that this did not work, I left the else part empty.
And also the cache was cleared + reloaded.
Does anyone have an idea what else I can do?

entry.title: Re: Actual Costing- T-code CKMLCP
entry.content: Check the table MLDOC，if the values of filed "WAERS" is not company code currency, change it as company code currency.

entry.title: Alert Management module
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I would like to use the Alert Management module in my SAP Business One 10.0 to create an Alerts Query. I already enabled the Alert Service checkbox and "Send Alert for Activities Scheduled for Today" in the General Settings but despite of this, Condition and Document Tab in Alert Management - Alert Details were still missing.

When I checked the predefined alerts, it has Condition and Documents Tabs though. Any ideas?



Sincerely,

Edzel.

entry.title: Re: Aggregation on Data Action
entry.content: William_Yu appreciate your response in relation to how it will be rectified

entry.title: What is AI Agent? Understanding Agentic AI with a simple and iterative approach
entry.content:
In 2025, a new buzzword is transforming the way we think about artificial intelligence: AI Agents or Agentic AI. This innovative approach moves away from traditional, one-shot AI models toward a more collaborative process where multiple specialized agents work together—much like a team of experts tackling a project. In this article, we explore what Agentic AI is, how it works, and the potential challenges it faces, while also suggesting visual aids to further enhance understanding.

What Is Agentic AI?

Traditional AI models, like the ones used in chatbots, operate by predicting one word at a time without revising their output. Imagine writing an entire essay without the ability to pause, review, or edit your work. Now, consider the difference when you draft a piece, receive feedback, and refine your text until it’s polished. This iterative human process is at the heart of Agentic AI.

Agentic AI breaks down complex tasks into specialized parts handled by different "agents," each performing a specific role:

 

  • Draft Agent: Generates the initial version of the content.
  • Critique Agent: Reviews the draft to identify areas for improvement.
  • Refinement Agent: Revises the content based on the critique to produce a final, polished product.

 

Beyond language models, some agents might use tools like search engines for gathering real-time data or calculators for performing precise computations. An orchestrator agent can even manage these processes dynamically, deciding the next steps based on evolving needs.

How Agentic AI Works: A Step-by-Step Approach

Consider a scenario where you need to create a marketing plan for a new product launch:

 

  1. Initial Draft: The Draft Agent produces a first version of the marketing plan, much like jotting down a rough draft on paper.
  2. Feedback Loop: Next, the Critique Agent examines the draft, offering detailed feedback—similar to a colleague suggesting improvements.
  3. Final Refinement: Finally, the Refinement Agent revises the original draft using the feedback, ensuring the final plan is comprehensive and well-organized.

 

This multi-agent workflow not only mimics human creative processes but also leads to higher-quality outcomes by iteratively refining the content.

Advantages of Agentic AI

 

  • Human-Like Collaboration: Just as we refine our work through feedback and revision, Agentic AI creates better results by using specialized agents that build on one another’s outputs.
  • Flexibility and Versatility: Not every agent has to be a language model. Some can be tools—like a search engine for pulling in updated data or a calculator for precise math—making the process more adaptable to different tasks.
  • Dynamic Workflows: An orchestrator agent can manage and adjust the workflow in real-time, allowing for a flexible process that can pause, reflect, and iterate as needed.

 

Challenges and Considerations

While Agentic AI offers exciting advantages, it also presents several challenges:

 

  • Coordination Complexity: Integrating multiple agents—each with its own specialized function—requires careful orchestration. Miscommunication between agents could lead to errors or inconsistencies.
  • Resource Overhead: Running several agents in tandem may demand more computational resources compared to a single-shot AI model, potentially impacting efficiency and cost.
  • Error Propagation: If one agent makes an error, there is a risk that this mistake could be amplified through the subsequent steps, affecting the final output.
  • Integration Challenges: Combining diverse tools (like search engines, calculation tools, and language models) into a seamless workflow can be technically challenging and may require robust oversight.

 

Recognizing these challenges is essential for researchers and organizations aiming to harness the full potential of Agentic AI.

 

Visualizing the Workflow

For many, a visual representation can make complex processes easier to understand. Imagine a flowchart where:

 

  • Each box represents a different agent (Draft, Critique, Refinement, etc.).
  • Arrows illustrate the flow of information and feedback between these agents.
  • Additional boxes for tools (like search or calculator agents) are integrated into the overall workflow.

 

Creating diagrams or infographics can be an excellent way to communicate how Agentic AI operates and how the agents interact dynamically.

Conclusion

Agentic AI represents a paradigm shift in artificial intelligence—one that embraces the collaborative, iterative processes familiar to human work. By breaking down tasks into specialized steps, it promises more refined, creative, and accurate outputs. However, the approach also comes with its own set of challenges, from coordination complexities to potential error propagation.

As we move further into 2025 and beyond, understanding both the strengths and limitations of Agentic AI will be crucial. Whether you’re a business leader, developer, or simply an enthusiast, keeping an eye on this evolving technology—and using visual tools to demystify it—will help you stay ahead in the fast-paced world of AI innovation.

Embracing Agentic AI means looking at AI not just as a single tool but as a dynamic team of agents working together to solve complex problems—paving the way for smarter, more human-like machine intelligence.

entry.title: Webinar 12/03: BTP Garage: Setup a SAP BTP Financial Operations Application

Join us on YouTube on Wednesday, March 12th 2025, for a discussion on:

entry.title: Re: Problem export pdf api Service Layer API
entry.content:
Hi,

 

you can use the API Gateway, guide on how to use it can be found here.

 

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Customizing Field Values – Part 1: How to Use Filtered List
entry.content:
User-Defined Values (UDVs) is a key feature introduced in SAP Business One Web client, release version 10.0 FP 2502. In this series of four blogs, we'll explain what this feature is and how to use it effectively. This first blog will get you started on how to use a filtered list to customize field values.

The UDVs feature enables you to customize values for a variety of fields in sales and purchasing documents. You can automate the manual feed of single values or tailor the values visible in a dropdown list. For example, you can set the delivery date of a sales order to automatically be one week after the posting date. Each time you enter a posting date, the delivery date will be set to one week later.

In version 10.0 FP 2502, the focus is on supporting marketing documents. However, stay tuned as we continuously expand our support to include more objects in the near future.

In order to define UDVs for a field, switch to the Create or Edit mode of a document, and then choose "Edit User-Defined Values" on the top right corner:

This action marks the fields that can be customized with a pencil icon. Choose the pencil icon to open the UDVs setup dialog.

Currently, you can use a filtered list or a formula to set up UDVs. In this blog, we’ll focus on the filtered list.

You can use a filtered list if you want to limit your users to a specific selection, ensuring they do not accidentally choose incorrect values.

For example, you have a UDF "Colors" with a dropdown list of all valid product colors in stock, and you want to sell only "Blue" and "White" colors in the coming week, you can use a filtered list to limit the options to just those two colors.

In the UDVs setup dialog, you can add "Blue" and "White" to a filtered list from the original list of colors. You can also choose whether to display all values in the dropdown list of the "Colors" field. This is useful if you want your users to have the option to switch between the narrowed list and the complete list while working with the values.

After you complete the setup, you can see an orange indicator around the "Colors" field with UDVs:

Watch this short video for a live demo.

In the next blog, we’ll explain how to use a formula to set up UDVs.

entry.title: Re: Working with RAP BO Events end-to-end - Event-driven-architecture - Event framework
entry.content: Hi @eachtime . If you read the blog carefully, I guide you where to click on the HUB in order to search for events. First your select your product, for example SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition. Then you have different tabs and can select Events - Event Objects. I also provided a screenshot for the same, if you check underneath 1.1

entry.title: SAP PO (Process Orchestration) to SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration) Migration
entry.content:
Motivation


SAP Process Orchestration (PO) supports custom process applications and integration scenarios for a lot of SAP customers. It evolved over many years to a very stable and feature rich integration solution. Because of changing business requirements and your IT strategy, it could be a good idea to check, if SAP Integration Suite supports your processes and integration requirements better?

If you consider moving to SAP Integration Suite, you might ask yourself what is the best time to start a project for your migration?

Benefits of SAP Integration Suite


SAP outlines some benefits why customers should migrate and transform with SAP BTP  / SAP Integration Suite:

  • SAP Integration Suite provides the flexibility and independence you need by delivering technology to move assets to the cloud, integrate the IT landscape, and configure and extend SAP and third-party applications.

     

    • Achieve seamless business processes: Integrate processes and data across diverse application landscapes and business networks with comprehensive integration capabilities.
      Accelerate business outcomes: Quickly integrate and connect any application or business partner with thousands of pre-built integrations using a proven methodology based on 50 years of SAP best practices.

       

    • Modernize integration: Enable self-service integration at scale with simpler tools, built-in AI, and modern integration capabilities. Deploy to any cloud with trust and security fully managed by SAP.

       

      SAP Assessment and Migration Tool


      It was possible to move to SAP Integration Suite in a - manual way - by migration SAP PI /PO IFlows and ICos to Integration Flows in CPI (former name of Cloud Integration) since years. By January 23 SAP released their SAP Assessment and Migration Tool to support migration projects. So is a migration from SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite now a "click of a button"? I think that is with the current version of the solution not possible, because some functionality is missing and a migration of the complete content will not be possible and sometime does not make sense. But you get a good overview on your integration content in SAP Process Orchestration with a rough estimation of your effort to move to SAP Integration Suite.

      The first step will be to connect you SA PO system via Cloud Connector in the Migration Assessment tool like described in SAP Help.

      After that you can start with extracting data from your system

       

       

       

       

      Extract Data in SAP Assessment Tool

       

      When the extraction job is finished, you can create a based on the data via:

       

       

       

       

       


      Scenario Evaluation


      This will show you a nice dashboard, but also the opportunity to download the results via Excel or PDF.

       

       

       

       

       

       

      Dashboard

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

      Download Results

       

      The next steps will be to evaluate the results and estimation and perhaps identify some scenarios, where you will have some quick wins or easy to migrate ones. If there are special requirements from your side, you can check the rules in the settings section.


      If you identified some flows, you would like to migrate, you are able to directly start with the Migration Tool. That is not designed as central tool with a central dashboard to push content to Cloud Integration but more as pulling flows from SAP PO to specific packages in Cloud Integration. So first of all, create a new package and the click on "Migrate":

       

       

       

       

       

       

      Migrate in Cloud Integration


      After selecting a flow and some more clicks, you migrated your first flow from SAP PO to Cloud Integration 

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

      first migrated flow


      For sure, you can do the migration manually, but at the end a tool does it in a reproducible und save way. There are some restrictions today, so that you need to do some adjustments manually. To make it more and more sures, I would suggest doing at least some testing and send feedback to SAP in case of issues or missing features. Some generic ideas and information’s can we found here.

      Please keep in mind, that there is no status update for migrated flows today. So consider to mark them in PO in an appropriate way.

      SAP Integration Suite vs. Cloud Integration


      Integration became more complex in comparison that was there, when SAP PO was designed. So I think a migration from SAP PO to Cloud Integration is not the whole story: There are much more holistic capabilities in SAP Integration Suite and it makes sense to perhaps rethink the implemented scenarios and to migrate from SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite, e.g. to:

      • add a central API proxy as entry point for more security or a better developer experience

         

      • use Event Mesh for high volume interface or to implement loose coupling
      • implement a CAP service for some central logic, that could be reused.

        Unit, Integration and Regression Testing
        In migration projects the implementation takes time, but typically testing is a a big block where you need integration and business experts. Because business experts have a lot do what delivers more values than doing tests manually, it could be very a booster for your project to automate testing as much as possible:

      • Unit test manually via simulation and test tool
      • Integration testing via INT4 Shield with PO and Cloud Integration in one step
      • Use integration tests for regressions tests also after migration project

        If you don't have solutions in place for that, consider to use the 12 month free license of the Figaf tool or the INT4 Shield.

        Migration Approaches
        When thinking a bit on migration options or strategies, I find a analogy to S/4 projects very interesting:

      • Brownfield conversion: More or less try to copy/migrate as much as possible content from SAP PO to Cloud Integration without changing the logic of implemented integrations. This could be a good option in case of many standard integrations or if are not able to change thing e.g. because of keeping migration costs low.
      • Selective: Copy the mass of interface in an intelligent way and implement changes, where it brings quick wins e.g. by using more flexible functionality compared to SAP PO. One idea could be to implement a API security and load balancing layer or to document your interfaces via API Hub Business Portal (OpenAPI)
      • Greenfield: Start with ISA-M process to design your Integration landscape of the future. After that, build some interfaces based on the new guidelines, learn about the new capabilities and how to handle them in live production. With these experiences build packages of interfaces and migrate them in waves to Integration. Perhaps you can use the best of PO and Integration for a while to have a smooth migration?

        Migration project steps / phases
        Perhaps we can reuse SAP Activate for our migration projects?

      • Discover: The outcome of the Discover phase is to lean about the new SAP Integration Suite capabilities an do define your Integration approach. Also, a basic documentation of your landscape should be ready afterwards.
      • Prepare: The outcome of the SAP Prepare phase is to setup the Assessment tool (see above) and to run the assessment on your SAP PO system(s). You should analyse the results in detail regarding effort and what is possible to migration automatically. During the Prepare phase, SAP or enterprise architects will build a business case for migration, which outlines the benefits and reasons for migrating from SAP PO to Integration Suite.
      • Explore: A change impact analysis will be completed to understand how SAP Integration Suite will benefit the business and integration team. This outcome of the Explore phase is to enable a smooth transition to the new system.
      • Realize: SAP functional consultants and business users will work together to ensure the objects in Integration Suite are delivered within the timeline and to a high standard. Once all the deliverables are satisfied, a cutover plan is made to move the configurations.
      • Deploy: The outcome of the Deploy phase is to migrate the interfaces to SAP Integration Suite so that customers and partners can benefit from the new capabilities while continuing to receive support in case of problems or concerns.
      • Run: The main task for consultants during the Run phase is to keep up to date with the latest innovation and technologies in SAP as part of the product’s “Continued Learning” philosophy. Once the transformed integration landscape is up and running, the focus shifts to continuous improvement and risk management.

        SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite Migration 
        So finally, what to do? Start now? Migrate everything in one step? Or do it step by step? What is the best way to do it and what is the best target architecture?

        I think it makes sense nearly for every customer to START NOW, because:

      • we now have the tools form SAP in GA
      • there are quick wins by SAP Integration Suite nearly for every scenario
      • it is a complex project, that takes time and will be become more difficult, when starting at the end of SAP PO lifetime

        For sure, there are still missing capabilities in the Migration- and Assessment tool and also in SAP Integration Suite (compared to SAP PO). That's we I would suggest starting now, to migrate what makes sense in you landscape and to use the power of SAP PO and SAP Integration perhaps for a certain time until needed features are available.

         

         

      •  

      •  

      •  

      •  

      •  

    •

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Mobile App - Timesheet to network codes/work orders functionality workaround
entry.content: Hi Justin, please can you share more details on the data type for specific network code / work order. Is it on the list of supported list of customer fields in mobile time sheet? https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_MOBILE/c3cbc8ddf8ef421d81d4b71a6f88a7a3/9ec6c201d4b6469caf7605c34f3364f0.html?locale=en-US#loio90246daa1e2f43f29a31855f7efe61cf

entry.title: Customizing Field Values – Part 2: How to Use Mathematical Formula
entry.content:
In previous blog, we saw how to setup UDV of filtered list. In this blog, we’ll explore how to use mathematical formulas to set values for numeric, text, and date controls.

Consider a common and simple use case: each time you enter the Posting Date of a document, you want the system to automatically set the Delivery Date to one week later.

The first step is to specify the trigger, which is a field that will initiate the formula calculation. In this case, the trigger can be either the Posting Date or the Customer Code field:

The second step is to specify the formula itself, which is pretty straightforward: PostingDate + 7. That's it! You can then test the formula to ensure it calculates the correct value.

Watch this short video for a live demo.

As a second example, assume in a sales order, you have a UDF to store the discount coupon value (in %) and another UDF to calculate the final price after applying the coupon. Each time you enter a coupon value, the final price is calculated automatically. Since the coupon field in the document grid rather than the header, you need to use the namespace "Product_Item". The formula will look like this:

Watch this short video for a live demo.

Here are a few key points about writing a formula:

  • Each field in current UI form is actually a variable that you can refer to in a formula. The variable name is quite similar to the GUI field name.
  • The variable can be sourced from the document header or document grid. To refer to a field in the header, simply start typing its name and the system will auto-compete it.
  • The formula supports mathematical operators and brackets (, +, -, *, /, (,)), as well as logical operators (which we’ll cover in the last blog of this series).
  • The formula also supports the addition of strings, e.g., “Contact Person is“ + ContactPerson

In the next blog, we’ll cover formulas which refer to fields in the database.

entry.title: Customizing Field Values – Part 3: How to Use Mathematical Formula with Database Fields
entry.content:
In the previous blog, we showed how to define a mathematical formula that uses only fields on the current UI. In this blog, we’ll dive deeper into defining a mathematical formula that refers to fields from the database.

Let’s consider a more complex yet common use case: each time you change the customer code in a sales order, you want the system to retrieve the customer record from the database and copy the customer's balance to the Remarks field in the sales order:

The first step is to specify the trigger, which is a field that will activate the formula. In this case, the trigger is the Customer Code field.

The second step is to set the lookup conditions. Since the balance field is stored in the database rather than on the current sales order UI, you need to use the lookup mechanism to retrieve the relevant record from the database according to a key taken from the current UI. In our example, to retrieve the relevant business partner record, you need to use the business partner code in the sales order. This is done using the lookup table:

The lookup object is Business Partner (first column in the table). The lookup field is Card Code (second column). The card code value is taken from the customer code in the sales order (last column).

The last step is defining the formula itself. To access a field in the database, type the namespace "db.". This will auto-complete all lookup objects that are defined in the table. The formula is pretty straightforward:

You can then test the formula to ensure it calculates the correct value.

Watch this short video for a live demo.

From a technical perspective, in the SAP Business One desktop client, defining such a UDV requires writing a SQL query. However, in the Web client, we eliminate the need to write a SQL query by breaking it down into a formula and a lookup field:

Key Points about the lookup table:

  • Lookup object: Based on Business Object (BO) names from the Service Layer. For your convenience, we also display the corresponding database table names if you are more familiar with it. For example, Contacts (OCLG).
  • Lookup field: Also derived from the BO names. We provide the corresponding database field names and descriptions as well. For example, CampaignNumber(CpnNo) – Campaign No.
  • Field name (compare with): Based on uname, a unique identifier similar to the GUI field name in the current form.
  • You can define several lookup objects for one formula, each with multiple lookup fields. All fields are collectively used to identify a single database record, with the system applying an AND operator to the fields during backend runtime.
  • Note that if you use the same field in the Lookup Field and Value/Field Name columns, it will appear in the dropdown lists with different names. This occurs because the lookup fields are named based on BO, while UI fields adopt a unique name (uname), similar to GUI labels, as seen in the example above, "CardCode" vs. "CustomerCode".

In summary, you can use a formula to reference fields either on the current UI or from the database and apply basic mathematical operators to them.

In the next and final blog, we’ll cover logical formulas.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Transportation Order Items custom field extension
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Greetings!

In public cloud Transportation Order Item app Unable to see custom field, after publishing custom field from SAP GUI.

Transportation Order Header able to see the custom field but same way unable to see item level.

so, please help me on this.

Thanks In Advance!

Regards,

entry.title: Customizing Field Values – Part 4: How to Use Logical Formula
entry.content:
In the previous blog, we demonstrated how to define a mathematical formula that uses fields from the database. In this blog, we’ll illustrate how to define a logical formula.

Using a similar concept as in the previous blog, this time the use case is: each time you change the customer code in a sales order, you want the system to retrieve the customer record from the database, check if the customer's balance is greater than 100,000$, and if so, automatically select a UDF checkbox that indicates the customer is a "Gold" customer.

 

The first step is to specify the trigger, which is a field that will activate the UDV formula. In this case, the trigger is the Customer Code field.

The second step is to set the lookup conditions, similar to the example of customer balance in the previous blog (see here for more details). This involves retrieving the business partner record based on the customer code in the sales order:

The final step is defining the formula itself, which is a simple logical expression:

The system will check at runtime whether the balance is greater than 100,000. Based on the result, the UDF checkbox value will be set accordingly: if true, the checkbox will be turned on; if not, it will be turned off.

Watch this short video for a live demo.

 Here are a few tips for writing logical formulas:

  • The logical formula supports logical operators and brackets (, <, >, !, ==, &&, ||, (,)), where && stands for the AND operator, || for the OR operator, ! for the NOT operator, and == for the EQUAL operator
  • If you refer to a Boolean variable in the formula (such as a checkbox) and you want to check its value (on/off), it is sufficient to specify only the field name; there is no need to compare it to true/false (although this is possible)
  • In the lookup table, if the lookup field is a Boolean and you want to compare it with a constant value, the constant value should be Y or N (Yes/No)

This blog completes the series on UDV. We hope you find it beneficial and useful.

entry.title: Recruiting Groups per legal entity
entry.content:
Hi,

We are using one job requisition template today, but in the SF suite we operate with different legal entities. I want to create one recruiting group per legal entity. I want each recruiting group to have access to job requisition that only applies to their respective legal entities. Today all recruiters see all job reqs. 

How can this be achieved? Any way to automate this?

Post Learning Events as Users or Supervisors with a Web Service | SAP Help Portal

As you can see in the sample, not the IDs are used but their descriptions. For example: not "04" but "PASSED" should be used.

Please have another try. 🙂 Good luck!

KR Jonas

entry.title: ChaRM Due by date is not copied to the change document from change request
entry.content:
We have configured customer specific transaction ZMCR and ZMCG. When approving change request ZMCR the change document ZMCG is created. Start date and requested end date are copied to change document but Due by date is empty in ZMCG change dokument

I have checked configuration Capabilities - change request Management - Make Settings for Change transaction types and Copy control Rules. 

In Map Date types we have only one mapping SDCD_RELE (implementation date) is mapped to SDCD_RELE

Should we have somethig more in that date mapping configuration?

entry.title: Re: CR for VS dropped after 2027?
entry.content:
Thanks Don for your reply.

You wrote: "And correct, as of December 2025 there will no longer be 32 bit runtime for distribution.."
So after the last SP for 32bit this December (let's say SP 39), only the 64bit runtime will continue to receive SPs.

So f.ex. in August 2026 we'll have 32bit with SP 39 and 64bit with SP 40. Can both versions be installed together on the same PC?

BTW. you're talking about differences between 32/64bit OS. But what is also important is the behavior of 32/64bit programs. A 64bit OS supports 32bit programs....

In August 2026, a 32bit program on a 64bit PC will use the 32bit CR-runtime with SP 39, another 64bit program will use the 64bit runtime SP40 - will this work on the same PC?

Regards,
Patrick

Tjank you all

entry.title: Re: In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA – Ledger Scoping Q&A
entry.content:
Dear Verena, 

In the public cloud and in our scenario, group reporting is to be carried out in accordance with HGB or DEAP. This means that we allocate as follows:

Ledger 0L --> Accounting Principle DEAP
Ledger 2L --> Accounting Principle local

The default for 0L was IFRS. Do we have to consider further configuration tasks so that the valuation approaches and regulations according to DEAP are applied consistently in 0L? Or did everything switch automatically with the assignment of DEAP to 0L?

Thank you very much
Sebastian

entry.title: SAP Premium Engagement Session - PE for RISE with SAP (March 27, 2025)
entry.content:
Virtual

Agenda

March 27, 2025

Dear Valued Customer,

We are pleased to invite you this year’s second SAP Premium Engagement Session focusing on the important topic “PE for RISE with SAP - How to Utilize PE and Bring Value to You”. This event is taking place virtually on March 27, 2025. Check out the detailed agenda here.

We continue these virtual Premium Engagements Sessions, as we can see many companies are very interested in this way of learning, networking, and sharing – after the March session, we have the next session in May covering “SAP Premium Engagement Services for Datasphere, SAP BTP & Security”. Watch out for the invitation.

In this March event about PE for RISE with SAP, we will be focusing on the increasing demand for RISE-solutions – especially migration to/implementation on RISE with SAP, the TQMs are getting more and more questions on how PE can support.

We will cover how to utilize PE services for RISE with SAP projects and the different feels (Brownfield, Greenfield, etc.). Join and be part of this exciting event. An expert speaker will be sharing valuable insights and there will be opportunity for raising your questions.

Don't miss this fantastic chance to gain practical insights and network with like-minded professionals who are embarking on a similar journey towards digital transformation. We invite you to join us and participate in this engaging discussion. Your presence would add great value to the event.

Take the chance to participate in this exclusive event by registering now with the link below. Feel free to share this invitation with your colleagues, there is no limit to how many can attend, and no cost.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Important

Please note that the 'Attend' button which can be found within this blog post does not serve as a formal registration for the event.

To officially register, please reach out to me, paul.schropp@sap.com and I will ensure that your registration is properly processed.

We look forward to having you join us for our upcoming events and thank you for your understanding!

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

Again thanks for sharing

Byron

entry.title: Re: translate the text in the acceptance Criteria
entry.content: ok thank you

entry.title: Re: C0 Developer Extensibility for CDS Behavior Definitions
entry.content:
Hi @WRoeckelein 

let me try to answer your question.

In general, the objects of the BO Interface need to be C1 released. These are the BDEF Interface and all CDS Projection View Entities belonging to the BDEF interface.

If you only want to allow BDEF extensibility, you need to make the BDEFs on all layers (base, interface, projection) extensible and release them for C0.

If you also want to allow Data Model Extensibility, you also have to make the corresponding CDS View Entities on all layers extensible and release them for C0. This step is also required, of node extensibility should be supported.

However, the link you have posted, refers to the extensibility of BDEF extensions. For BDEF extensions, or extensions in general, it is important to know, the they inherit its API state from the core object. So, if you e.g. add a new node in a BDEF extension and mark it as extensible, you do not need to set a release state for the extension(s). What you need to do, is to set the release state for the additional CDS View Entities of the new node. So C1, on the interface and C0 on all layers, if the new CDS View Entity should be extensible.

Hope this clarifies you questions. Let me know if there is anything left.

Best regards,

Daniel

Lead Architect - RAP Extensibility

entry.title: Re: "I am not able to take the 'Stay Certified' assessment.
entry.content: I have the same issue with C_TS413. Please provide guidance since 01.04.2025 is coming close.

entry.title: Mapping company codes for the replication of the workforce data from SAP MDI to S4 Hana
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

 

We are currently starting an implementation of S4 Public Cloud for one of our customers with SAP MDI and SAP SuccessFactors.

 

On the starter system (XXX) we have configured replication of workforce from SuccessFactors to S4. While all the employees are arriving with errors:

 

"Company code YYYY has not been created in financial accounting."

 

Because the company codes are different in the SF and S4.

 

Could you please tell me what can be suitable approach to map the company codes for the incoming employees from SAP MDI? And also outgoing Cost Centers to SAP MDI?

 

Is there a way to maintain a value mapping? Or it needs to be done via BAPI?

 

Thanks a lot!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Mapping company codes for the replication of the work...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

To replicate the Company Code from SF to S4 Hana using MDI, you need to follow the configuration of Company code replication before triggering any data from MDI.

Kindly check on the below link for the details regarding replication of the company code.

https://help.sap.com/docs/intelligent-enterprise/mdi-cross-product-guide/company-code-replication-s4hc?locale=en-US

 

Thanks and Regards,
Moumita.

Is it possible to merge them instead of having a duplication of tabs with the same name ?

 

Thanks in advance

 

@Vera_Gutbrod @ThomasReiss

HI Experts, 

In ECC and S4H Private EVO / EFB parameter IDs can be well set. But not in Public, where I can not find those possibilities, nor the user parameters; For instance used-to-be-EVO configuration, only have one option: the inforecord creation automation.

Could you, please, let me know, if these objects are disabled in Public, or any other way to handle such cases, where used to use EVO / EFB

Maria

entry.title: Re: Transfer Stock Using Manual Reservation
entry.content:
Hello @Charisma_fitra 

As per note 28692 - MMIM: Reservation for a two-step stock transfer you need to use stock transport orders to plan 2-step transfers, as reservations are not supported for 2-step transfers.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

If we enable the downgrade protection or cross reference check, but it is available only for prod system.

How we can handle the TR sequencing part in non prod system (Quality/Pre-prod)?

Sneha

entry.title: How to Create " Insert Sections" in SAP SAC Tables (Similar to Webi?)
entry.content:
In SAP Web Intelligence (Webi), the "Insert Section" option allows us to split a table into separate sections based on a selected dimension. However, I can't find a direct equivalent of this feature in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

Scenario:

  • My dataset includes Business Unit and Employees as dimensions, along with Few measures.
  • The number of Business Units and Employees changes dynamically.
  • In Webi, I could use "insert section" option on Business Unit, which would automatically create separate tables for each Business Unit available, displaying its employees and measures.
  • In SAC, I need a way to achieve the same result—showing all Business Units simultaneously in separate sections—without using filters, as the business requirement is to see all Business units at once.

My Requirement is ; How can I replicate Or Achieve Insert Section functionality in SAC to dynamically display separate tables for each Business Unit?

entry.title: Re: Aggregation on Data Action
entry.content:
Though I'm not sure why the multiply should be after aggregation, but if that's the logic, should try to use variable in advance formula to get the aggretated result first.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/afe93e3cf1414a7b8419baad11cc066e.html#loio940ed44080b947098210a927043d19f9you

entry.title: Re: Recruiting Groups per legal entity
entry.content:
Hi,

This link of the SAP Help Portal page you can see how to create and edit Recruiting Groups.

Here’s a step-by-step approach to configuring this setup:

  1. Define Recruiting Groups:
    • Create Recruiting Groups for each legal entity. Recruiting Groups are clusters of users that share the same permissions or access rights to job requisitions.
    • Go to "Admin Center" -> "Manage Recruiting Groups" and create a group for each legal entity.
  2. Assign Users to Recruiting Groups:
    • Assign recruiters to the appropriate Recruiting Group based on their legal entity. This way, they will only see and access job requisitions associated with their group.
    • You can automate this aspect via user import functionalities or APIs, ensuring users are dynamically placed into the correct groups based on attributes like “Legal Entity”.
  3. Modify Job Requisition Templates:
    • Ensure that job requisition templates include a field or attribute distinguishing which legal entity they apply to.
    • This can be done by adding a custom field to the template or utilizing an existing field like “Location” or “Division” to indirectly represent the legal entity.
  4. Set Permission Rules:
    • Adjust the Permission Rules so that each Recruiting Group has access restrictions based on legal entity identifiers.
    • Configure permission roles in “Admin Center” -> “Manage Permission Roles”, and define the criteria using either a custom field (representing legal entity) or leveraging existing fields like "Department" or "Location".
  5. Automating Access Control:
    • Create dynamic permissions using “Manage Permission Groups” to ensure permissions adapt based on changes to user attributes (e.g., if a recruiter switches legal entities).

Additional information can be found in KBA 2291548 - Working with Recruiting Groups in SuccessFactors Recruiting.

I hope about information help you

Best regards

Alejandro

entry.title: 5 Key Questions Answered by SAP Discovery Center
entry.content:
When you're looking to integrate, extend, optimize processes, maximize data, and use AI in your SAP cloud solutions, you might find yourself asking similar questions:

  • Where do I start?
  • What’s available?
  • How do I integrate everything?
  • How can I upskill my team?
  • What’s possible and what will it cost?

The good news is, SAP Discovery Center has the answers! This free resource provides hands-on guidance, best practices, comprehensive catalogs, and estimators for SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) Services and SAP Business AI Features. Here are the top five questions SAP Discovery Center helps answer:

1. What SAP BTP Services and AI Features are available, and what do they cost?

SAP BTP provides the foundation for SAP Business AI and SAP Business Data Cloud. It’s the go-to platform for building, integrating, and extending SAP solutions, offering a variety of services:

  • App Dev & Automation: Tools to build AI-powered extensions that streamline business processes.
  • Data & Analytics: Solutions like SAP HANA Cloud for real-time data processing.
  • Integration: Services to seamlessly connect SAP and non-SAP applications.
  • Artificial Intelligence: Technologies such as SAP AI Core and Document Information Extraction.

The SAP Discovery Center provides a detailed catalog of all SAP BTP Services and AI Features, complete with use cases, implementation guidance, and roadmaps to help you choose the right tools for your needs.

Want to know the cost and value of these services? The AI Feature Catalog provides a clear overview of each AI feature, including benefits, setup guidance, and built-in cost and value calculations via the AI Estimator. You can even create and share estimates directly from the platform. The same estimator tool is available for SAP BTP services with the Service Estimator.

2. How can I get started quickly?

Innovation requires speed. SAP Discovery Center simplifies getting started with:

  • Missions: Step-by-step guides to address specific business challenges.
  • Reference Architectures: Ready-to-use templates and best practices for designing your SAP BTP solution architecture.
  • Business Accelerator Content: Pre-built integration content, SAP Build, and analytics to fast-track your implementation.
  • Quick Account Setup & Free Trials: Get hands-on experience without upfront costs through SAP BTP Free Trial or Free Tier options.

With these resources, you can start quickly and scale efficiently without spending weeks on research.

3. Where can I find practical guidance for real business challenges?

Learning by doing is the way to go! SAP Discovery Center helps you build expertise and solve real business challenges with:

  • Missions: Structured Project Boards with phases and exercises simulating real-world implementations.
  • Documentation & Tutorials: Deep dives into specific services and tasks, available in both missions and individual service pages.
  • Expert Support: Some missions provide direct engagement with SAP experts, and you can always tap into the SAP Community for advice and tips.

Continuous learning keeps your team ahead of the curve, helping you explore innovations and implement solutions faster.

4. Which SAP BTP services do I need, and how do they work together?

SAP BTP services are built to interact with each other and integrate with both SAP and non-SAP applications. SAP Discovery Center helps you align your system landscape through:

  • Reference Architectures: Proven templates and best practices for solution architectures, speeding up delivery and enhancing reliability.
  • Missions: Use cases with detailed architecture diagrams and a breakdown of required services and components.

5. How can I push the limits of standard SAP solutions?

Every business is unique and sometimes a tailored solution is the best fit for your use case. Whether you’re exploring SAP BTP and SAP Business AI for the first time or optimizing existing solutions, SAP Discovery Center offers the tools, guidance, and expert insights to support you every step of the way. Browse the catalogs to find the inspiration and motivation to make a real difference for your business.

Ready to Get Started?

Visit SAP Discovery Center today to discover, evaluate, and adopt SAP BTP services and AI features that empower you to create tailored business solutions.

You May Also Be Interested In:

Joining one of our SAP BTP Garage webinars, where we demonstrate practical learning through real-world use cases and hands-on demos. Each episode focuses on a specific SAP Discovery Center Mission to provide valuable insights.

entry.title: Re: Revenue recognition service order type RP01 and RP02
entry.content:
Hello,

As of March 2025, event-based revenue recognition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud currently does not support repair orders like RPO1 and RPO2. Although you see them listed in filters within certain apps, the functionality to post event-based revenue recognition for these types of repair orders is not available yet.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: LMS - How does the "Remind recipients if no due date...
entry.content:
also, you seem to need to have an assignment type filled in to have the notification sent - sometimes it's not set to mandatory or optional....

The notifications include pending assignments, both required and non-required, that are assigned to learners without specified due dates for completion.

entry.title: Re: CAP Hybrid testing using PostgreSQL on SAP BTP, hyper...

@valentincadart 
The following question is on the same topic using CAP Java. I was able to connect to PostgreSQL instance from BAS using the method described in the answer.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/cap-java-how-to-connect-to-postgresql-on-btp-from-bas/qaq-p/14003400

entry.title: Re: Process Goverannce Issues: One with "Modeler" role an...
entry.content:
Hi TNichols,

Your process stops during the second execution of the activity for the modeler because the next element is a parallel gateway. As you can see, the gateway has three incoming sequence flows (arrows) and expects data from all three before proceeding. In the second execution, only one sequence flow provides input to the gateway. Since the gateway does not recognize that only one input is expected, it waits for all three—resulting in a deadlock. Here you can read more about the topic. 

To avoid this behavior, I recommend removing the parallel gateways and executing the activities sequentially. This approach simplifies loop creation, as intended in your example. Parallel gateways should only be used when there are no additional interactions with the affected process part.

Regarding your second issue: While you correctly assigned tasks using the modeler, this currently acts only as a placeholder without actual data. You have two options to populate the modeler:

1.Create a Modeler Group in your organizational settings and add modelers from your unit. When the task “Add Operational Metric” arises, they can choose to complete it.

2.Assign a Specific Modeler by retrieving information from the model using the SAP Signavio Process Manager API and mapping the user to the respective SAP Signavio Process Governance user. Here are two useful resources to get started with the SPM, SPG). You would use a Java Script action to do so. 

For your third question: It seems that the issue appears only with the Case Creator role. This is likely because not all cases have a designated creator—for example, in automatically started workflow instances. If you select any other role, the red dot should not appear.

I hope this helps.

entry.title: How to trigger the defect recording and notification creation by using the API?
entry.content:
I tried to pass the result from external system to SAP by using the API: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/a08e12a754cf4891b41a01a285d065bb/11aead0522e1401485770fb0fba9d082.html?q=API%20for%20quality%20management&locale=en-US

I noticed that it mentioned in the page that I should be able to create the defect automatically:

 

Now the result is created successfully for my inspection lot. However, the defect and notification is not created.

I tired with the same data in the front-end and it works.

  • Notification type is assigned to the target inspection type
  • In the assigned inspection plan, there is only one characteristic with control indicator "Defects recording" ticked and "Defect codes for rejection" assigned.
  • During the result recording, the status is updated to '5', valuation is given with "Rejected"

Do you have any idea why it doesn't work with API? Did I miss anything?

 

Thank you!

What can I do that they are no longer saved there?

entry.title: Re: How to generate your SAP Datasphere documentation aut...
entry.content: Hi PMathieu, can you little please elaborate how to do this,if you can include the steps .it will be helpful.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to add the source of the journal entry?
entry.content:
Hi @dspark ,

One suggestion is to create a Transaction Code and assign it to your revaluations as below:

Then the resulting Journal Entry would have a clear reference of its origin. Additionally, you can use TransactionNotification to block the usage of this Transaction Code for other purposes and guarantee accuracy in reports.

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

*** Please, if this answer resolves your question, kindly consider marking it as accepted. This helps our community by highlighting useful solutions. Thank you! ***

entry.title: Re: "I am not able to take the 'Stay Certified' assessment.
entry.content: I just checked, and now it is working

entry.title: Users analytics in SAP CDC
entry.content:
Hey there,

 

we are using SAP CDC to manage user identities and the whole login process on our website.

Looking for reporting opportunities regarding user actions.

I only find this "Partner Dashboard" section in our CDC.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Users analytics in SAP CDC
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

You can use 3 application in the console for analytical purposes. 

The Reports section provides information about users.

  • User Identities - shows reports for New Registered Users, New Users by Provider, Daily Logins, and Logged-in Users by Provider. For details, see User Identities Reports.
  • Identity Query Tool - dynamic query tool for searching and retrieving users' profile data and metadata. For details, see Identity Query Tool.
  • Customer Insights - generates useful analytics on users, including demographics, interests, social behavior, influence, and revenue-generating activities. For details, see Customer Insights.

 

Best Regards,

SAP Solution Support

entry.title: Re: Missing ODP_BW, ODP_SAPI, and SLT in Replication Flow...
entry.content: It is not possible to install DMIS on S/4 HANA -

entry.title: Re: Missing ODP_BW, ODP_SAPI, and SLT in Replication Flow (SAP S/4HANA to SAP Datasphere)
entry.content:
• Can you please provide more information?
  • What S/4 HANA Source environment are you using?
  • What connection Type are you using?
  • Have you installed all required notes?

    Accessing tables in S/4 HANA via SLT is not encouraged - use CDS Views instead.

entry.title: Re: Download button for Bonus Assignment statement in Peo...
entry.content: Any update on this? I'd be interested to know if this can be solved. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Payment Wizard didn't generate any documents
entry.content:
Hi @Tiago_Goncalves ,

Is this happening only to a specific BP?

In a positive case, I'd check BPs with the same currency and payment method assigned and see if they behave similarly.

If you're able to reproduce this in a test environment, that would be even better.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: SAP Joule - Identity Provisioning - No assigned Roles displayed
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Over the past few days, we have set up SAP Joule in our Public Cloud environment. This has worked well. But now we have a problem with the assigned roles in identity provisioning. When I search for a user, I get the message that there are now assigned roles for each user.

The problem now is that Joule doesn't show any results when searching for a specific app or anything else because the rules or permissions are missing. Surely the users in the public cloud have the specific roles assigned.

As additional information, we have an IAS and an IPS tenant in Cloud Identity Services.

Has anyone had the same problem so far?

Thanks for your help in advance.

Greetings Tobias

Joule SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP Cloud Identity Services

entry.title: Re: Background job details
entry.content:
Hi Mahil,

Use the transaction code SM37 to find the following details 

JobName, Job Created, Status, start date, time, Duration, Delay time.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing - SFC WC POD View
entry.content: Please check that this option  is selected in your material:

entry.title: disable RBA rule for particular users
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support team 


I would like to have your support in this case we are trying to disable the MFA rules for particular users in our live system without effecting the existing users or rules. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: disable RBA rule for particular users
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

You can create and maintain Default Account Rule-set.

Accounts rule-sets are rules that apply to individual accounts.

 

Best Regards,

SAP Solution Support

entry.title: Hungary Audit report: how to run the report in Public cloud without Sales invoice
entry.content:
Hello Everybody,

When we run the DRC Hungary Audit report, it generate nothing with the Error message" Element szamla occurs 0 times. Number of occurrences must be >= 1".I've check the best practice document , it said that "In this activity, the Audit Report based on Sales and Distribution (SD) invoices displays and downloads". But we have not implemented the SD module, all sales invoice are from maunal posting.

In this way, how can we use the Hungary audit report? 

In addintion,can anyboby help describe the difference abount the HungaryAudit Report and Hungary Real-Time Invoice Reporting ?

Many thanks!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

entry.title: Re: SAP DM Plugin and production process sync call
entry.content: Can you amend in the url this "&async=false" and it start to work

entry.title: Re: Base ratio is calculated on the Production Order row...
entry.content: Hi. 8 / 1505 = 0.0053156146.... So it is considering your components structure and calculating the equivalence.
